channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UEFA Sanctions Tottenham After Fans Make Nazi Salutes During Champions League Match
item.description: UEFA don fine Tottenham Hotspur and give the club one-year suspended ban from selling away tickets after three of their supporters make Nazi salutes during Champions League match against Eintracht Fra...

item.title: Over 30,000 people affected as water supply is cut in Nyeri, Nyandarua, and Laikipia counties
item.description: Residents of Nyeri, Nyandarua, and Laikipia counties are facing severe water shortages after the Water Resource Authority (WRA) disconnected the supply from the Matitu Water Project. The disconnection...

item.title: UK Organises First Repatriation Flight from Oman for British Nationals Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The UK government has arranged its first official repatriation flight from Muscat, Oman, for British citizens wishing to leave the Middle East due to escalating regional tensions. The flight, schedule...

item.title: PM urges leaders to continue educating citizens on land management and peace
item.description: Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba has urged local MPs and district leaders in Manyara Region, specifically from Mbulu, to continue educating citizens on land management to prevent conflicts and promo...

item.title: Israeli Strike in Qom Leaves Iranian Clerics Vulnerable Amid Khamenei Succession Uncertainty
item.description: An Israeli airstrike targeted a meeting location in Qom, Iran, where senior clerics of the Assembly of Experts were reportedly planning to discuss succession plans following the death of Supreme Leade...

item.title: Over 720,000 Tanzanian Youth Receive CRDB’s 20 Billion Loan to Support Businesses
item.description: Since 2023, CRDB Bank has provided a loan of over 20 billion Tanzanian Shillings to help 720,000 youth across the country. This funding is meant to serve as capital for young people starting businesse...

item.title: KSI Invests in Dagenham & Redbridge with Goal to Reach Premier League
item.description: YouTuber and entrepreneur KSI has purchased a minority stake in Dagenham & Redbridge Football Club, a team currently struggling in the sixth tier of English football. The club, which has faced relegat...

item.title: Trump pledges support for oil and gas shipping through Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged to ensure the safe passage of oil and natural gas shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint, after Israeli and U.S. strikes on Iran...

item.title: Podcast discussion with Mike Dorrell on Wall Street trends and AI
item.description: In this special edition of the Chanticleer podcast, hosts James Thomson and Anthony Macdonald engage with Mike Dorrell, named Financial Review Business Person of the Year 2025, live from the Australia...

item.title: Ajit Pawar Faces Controversy Over Disputed Pune Land Deal Involving Son Parth
item.description: Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Ajit Pawar is facing a new controversy as a multi-crore disputed land deal in Pune, allegedly involving his son Parth Pawar, has come to light. This latest developmen...

item.title: Tanzania’s Transport Sector Improvements Boost Government Revenue by 12 Trillion Shillings
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has highlighted the positive transformation in Tanzania's transport sector, particularly in its ports. Major investments have enabled Tanzanian ports to handle large vess...

item.title: Immigration Boss Evelyn Cheluget Faces Passport Scandal After RSF Ties Revealed
item.description: Evelyn Cheluget, Kenya’s Director-General of Immigration Services, finds herself under scrutiny after it was revealed that a key figure in Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces (RSF) holds a Kenyan passport. A...

item.title: Kashmir Shuts Schools Till March 7 Amid Protests Over Khamenei's Killing
item.description: The Kashmir region has been facing unrest following the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei, a prominent Iranian figure, leading to widespread protests. In response to the volatile situation, the Jammu and ...

item.title: New Order Investments confirms cooperation following Ormonde building collapse
item.description: The owners of the Ormonde building in south Johannesburg, which tragically collapsed on Monday, killing 12 workers, have publicly affirmed their cooperation with ongoing investigations. The collapse h...

item.title: Security Forces Eliminate Four Terrorists in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Operations
item.description: Pakistan's security forces have successfully neutralised four terrorists linked to the Indian-backed extremist group Fitna al-Khawarij in operations conducted across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa from November 1...

item.title: PNP to sustain energy-saving practices in light of ongoing global tensions
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has announced that it will continue to implement energy-saving measures across its operations in response to the ongoing global oil price fluctuations. This follow...

item.title: Kiiza and Mugo Headline PFL Africa Season Two Opener in Pretoria
item.description: The Professional Fighters League (PFL) Africa is set to return to South Africa with its Season Two opener on 10 April 2026 at the SunBet Arena in Menlyn Maine, Pretoria. The card will feature an excit...

item.title: Surge in Young Men Reaching Out for Gambling Help After Ontario Privatizes Online Betting
item.description: A new study reveals a sharp increase in the number of young men, particularly those aged 15 to 24, contacting Ontario's ConnexOntario gambling helpline for assistance with gambling-related issues. The...

item.title: Security and Unity Highlight Jamhuri Day Celebrations Across Kenyan Counties
item.description: This year's Jamhuri Day celebrations saw a mixture of emotions and discussions across Kenya's counties, with a significant focus on security and national unity. As Kenyans gathered to commemorate the ...

item.title: An Analysis of Donald Trump’s Economic Claims Against Current US Data
item.description: In his February 2026 State of the Union address, US President Donald Trump declared the nation to be in a 'golden age', claiming it was 'bigger, better, richer and stronger' than ever. While inflation...
